objects.name: PAP
objects.description: This defines PAP as a STIX extension

objects.name: Tycoon2FA PhaaS Operation
objects.description: Tycoon2FA is a massive Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform using Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) techniques to bypass MFA for Microsoft 365 and Gmail. It sends tens of millions of messages monthly, targeting over 500,000 organizations.

objects.content:
Sector: Cross-sector

Modus operandi:
AiTM proxy, session cookie theft, MFA bypass, heavy code obfuscation, custom CAPTCHAs, and Telegram exfiltration.

objects.name: Zero-Day Mass Exploitation
objects.description: Clop (Lace Tempest) continues its strategy of exploiting zero-day vulnerabilities in Managed File Transfer (MFT) solutions like Cleo (CVE-2024-50623), MOVEit, and GoAnywhere for large-scale data extortion.

objects.name: CVE-2024-50623
objects.description: Vulnerability CVE-2024-50623 referenced in threat report.

objects.content:
Sector: Technology, Finance, Healthcare

Modus operandi:
Exploitation of MFT zero-days, SQL injection, unrestricted file upload, deployment of 'LEMURLOOT' web shell, and data-only extortion via torrents.

objects.name: Silver Dragon / MonikerLoader
objects.description: The Silver Dragon group (linked to APT41) targets government entities in Europe and SE Asia using MonikerLoader and BamboLoader to deliver Cobalt Strike beacons via server exploitation and phishing.

objects.content:
Sector: Government

Modus operandi:
Public-facing server exploitation, phishing, Windows service hijacking for persistence, Google Drive-based C2 (GearDoor backdoor).

objects.name: CVE-2026-22719
objects.description:
A command injection vulnerability in the support-assisted product migration workflow allows unauthenticated remote code execution.

Affected product: VMware Aria Operations

Affected vendor: Broadcom

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:broadcom:vmware_aria_operations:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

objects.name: CVE-2026-21385
objects.description:
Integer overflow in the graphics component leading to memory corruption. Flagged by Google for targeted exploitation in Android devices.

Affected product: Graphics Subcomponent (Multiple Chipsets)

Affected vendor: Qualcomm

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:qualcomm:graphics_subcomponent:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

objects.name: CVE-2026-20079
objects.description:
Critical authentication bypass in the web interface allowing unauthenticated attackers to obtain root access to the OS.

Affected product: Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC)

Affected vendor: Cisco

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:cisco:secure_firewall_management_center:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

objects.name: CVE-2026-28289
objects.description:
Zero-click RCE via email attachment. Attackers use zero-width Unicode characters to bypass filename validation filters (Patch bypass for CVE-2026-27636).

Affected product: FreeScout Helpdesk

Affected vendor: FreeScout

CPE: cpe:2.3:a:freescout:freescout:*:*:*:*:*:*:*:*

objects.name: Breach: University of Hawaiʻi Cancer Center
objects.description:
A ransomware attack occurring in August 2025 (disclosed/updated March 2026) exposed the personal data of 1.2 million individuals, including SSNs and health-related research data.

Sector: Healthcare/Education

objects.name: Breach: HungerRush
objects.description:
Threat actors compromised an employee's device (infostealer) or a third-party vendor's credentials to gain access to HungerRush's email marketing service, leading to extortion emails being sent to millions of restaurant customers.

Sector: Retail/Food

objects.name: Breach: LexisNexis Legal & Professional
objects.description:
Breach of AWS infrastructure via the 'React2Shell' vulnerability in an unpatched React app. Approximately 2GB of legacy data and 21,000 account details were stolen.

Sector: Legal Services

objects.abstract: Geopolitical analysis (EN) / Middle East Military & Cyber Escalation
objects.content:
Sector: Energy, Critical Infrastructure, Government

Theme: Middle East Military & Cyber Escalation

Following US-Israeli strikes (Operation Epic Fury/Lion's Roar) on Iranian sites, a massive cyber spillover is observed. Iranian actors are targeting IP cameras (Hikvision/Dahua) across the Gulf, Israel, and Cyprus for battle damage assessment. A nationwide internet blackout in Iran (connectivity at 1%) is reported.

Tags: Iran, Israel, USA, Middle-East-Conflict, Hybrid-Warfare

objects.name: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Proliferation
objects.description:
A highly sophisticated set of 23 iOS exploits targeting versions 13.0 to 17.2.1. Initially used for state-sponsored surveillance, it is now being utilized by financial actors (UNC6691) for cryptocurrency theft.

Analysis:
The transition of Coruna from espionage to mass-scale crime represents a dangerous trend in exploit accessibility. The kit features PAC bypasses, sandbox escapes, and kernel privilege escalation.

Recommendations:
- Update iOS to the latest version immediately (iOS 18+).
- Enable Lockdown Mode for high-risk individuals.

objects.name: LastPass Spoofed Support Alerts
objects.description:
Phishing campaign spoofing LastPass support via 'verify-lastpass.com'. Emails claim unauthorized access or vault change requests to steal master passwords.

Analysis:
The campaign leverages 'display name spoofing' to trick users on mobile clients where the full sender address is hidden.

Recommendations:
- Remind users that master passwords are never requested by support.
- Report suspicious emails to abuse@lastpass.com.

objects.content: The threat landscape for March 2026 is characterized by a high-intensity fusion of kinetic and cyber warfare in the Middle East, alongside significant developments in mobile exploitation and the professionalization of phishing-as-a-service. The ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict has triggered massive cyber operations, including strategic targeting of critical infrastructure (IP cameras, control systems) for battlefield assessment. Simultaneously, the discovery of the 'Coruna' iOS exploit kit highlights the migration of spyware-grade tools to financial cybercriminals. Law enforcement remains active, notably with the seizure of the Leakbase forum. Organizations face increased risk from sophisticated AiTM (Adversary-in-the-Middle) phishing campaigns (Tycoon2FA) and critical zero-day vulnerabilities in management platforms (Cisco, VMware).
